dots / html skill

Component atlas

Every component in the dots HTML catalog, rendered as a live specimen with its real example markup and scoped styles. Use it to look up composition and rhythm before assembling an artifact — read the registry entry for the copy-paste source.

On this page

Page shellTOC rail Stat tilesCallout Data tableProcess steps Bar chartSparkline RecommendationCode panel Diff blockComparison grid DisclosureTimeline Flow diagramWide figure Evidence galleryPull quote Theme toggle
On this page
Page shellTOC rail Stat tilesCallout Data tableProcess steps Bar chartSparkline RecommendationCode panel Diff blockComparison grid DisclosureTimeline Flow diagramWide figure Evidence galleryPull quote Theme toggle

Page shell

Every page starts here. It's the static document skeleton — a context line, title, optional single status chip, and dek at the top, plus a footer slot for sources at the bottom. This atlas page is itself built on this shell and adds page-behavior separately.

project / area

Document title

draft

One-or-two-sentence dek: what this document is and the single decision or understanding it exists to drive.

Variants: none — the shell is invariant; vary the content, not the frame.

TOC rail

A sticky margin rail for six or more sections, or long reference pages readers navigate non-linearly. It docks in the right margin at wide viewports and becomes a compact native disclosure on narrow ones; short utilitarian pages omit it.

On this page
Section one Section twoSection three Section fourSection five Section six

Variants: right-docked (default) or left-docked.

Stat tiles

An instrument panel of two to five headline numbers a reader should absorb before any prose — counts, deltas, budgets, scores. Values stay short figures with the words carried by the label; a quieter third line can hold a source or comparison.

14
PRs merged
+3 vs last week
6
deploys
1
incident
SEV-2 · 47m
99.1%
suite pass rate

Variants: 4-up (default), 3-up, 2-up.

Callout

A fact the reader must not scroll past — a gotcha, a genuine warning, a hard blocker. It's a full hairline border with a faint tint, never a left accent stripe, and opens with a bold run-in word instead of a heading.

Note. The migration renames the config key; old configs keep working through v3 via the compatibility shim.

Alpha spec. The upstream schema is still changing — contain any dependency on it to the dev-time generator.

Blocked. Staging credentials expired; nothing below the deploy step has been verified.

Variants: note (default), warn, danger.

Data table

Structured rows a reader compares line by line — decisions, inventories, risk registers. It builds on theme.css's base table styles with a labeled first column, right-aligned numeric columns, and a horizontal scroll wrapper for wide content; dividers stay horizontal only, never a full grid.

Risk Owner Likelihood Exposure Mitigation
Vendor SSO deprecation Priya N.High $180k Migrate to OIDC before Sept 30 shutdown.
Single on-call for billing Marcus T.Medium $40k Cross-train two engineers this quarter.
Unpinned build image Release eng.Medium $15k Pin digest, add drift alert.
Data export lacks audit log Priya N.Low $25k Ship logging with v2.4, tracked in DATA-118.
Regional failover untested SREHigh $210k Run game day before next release freeze.

Variants: plain (default), first-column-labeled, numeric columns, or stacked text rows on narrow screens.

Process steps

A linear sequence of two to six stages that stays fully visible at every width. It reads horizontally on larger screens and becomes a connected vertical list on mobile; use the SVG flow diagram only when the path branches.

  1. 1
    Collect evidence

    Inspect the source and current behavior.

  2. 2
    Make the change

    Keep the correction scoped.

  3. 3
    Verify

    Render at the target widths.

  4. 4
    Hand off

    Deliver the evidence.

Variants: numbered (default) or compact without detail lines.

Bar chart

Horizontal bars for comparing magnitudes across up to about eight items — throughput by service, spend by team, latency by region — when the shape of the comparison matters more than exact digits. One bar can be emphasized in accent color; the fill grows once on scroll into view.

p95 latency by service (ms)
checkout-api
412
catalog-api
190
search
255
auth
104
notifications
78

Variants: default (quiet fills) or one bar with `.bar-fill.emphasis`.

Sparkline

A trend shown inline with a sentence or inside a stat tile, where the shape of change over time matters more than any single reading. No axes or grid — just a quiet line with one accent-colored dot marking the current value.

Weekly signups have climbed steadily since the referral program launched: 312/wk — up from 96/wk eight weeks ago.

Variants: inline (default) or embedded in a stat tile.

Recommendation

The document's committing conclusion — the moment it stops presenting options and states what to do. Placed once, near the end, after the supporting evidence, in a plain hairline-bordered card with no tint, stripe, or chip.

Ship the OIDC migration in Q3, ahead of the vendor's September shutdown. The current SSO integration has no fallback once the vendor deprecates its legacy auth endpoint, and the two-week buffer we'd have if we start in Q4 isn't enough to catch a failed rollout before the cutoff.

Assign it to Priya's team, who already own the auth service and scoped the OIDC client work last sprint. Freeze unrelated auth changes for the two weeks around cutover.

Variants: single (default) — one per document.

Code panel

Multi-line code or config the reader needs verbatim, on a dark surface that stays dark in both light and dark page modes so code always reads as code. Wide lines scroll horizontally inside the panel rather than widening the page.

retry.config.ts
// exponential backoff for the ingest worker
export const retryConfig = {
  maxAttempts: 5,
  baseDelayMs: 200,
  maxDelayMs: 10000,
  jitter: "full",
  retryOn: ["ETIMEDOUT", "ECONNRESET", 429, 503],
  onExhausted: function (job) {
    deadLetter.push(job);
  },
};

Variants: default (filename header) or omit the header for an anonymous fragment.

Diff block

A before/after code change worth showing inline — a fix, a migration step, a config change — where added and removed lines carry the point. Marks are a background tint plus a +/- glyph in a fixed-width gutter, kept short at around fifteen lines or fewer.

retry.config.ts
 export const retryConfig = {
-  maxAttempts: 3,
+  maxAttempts: 5,
-  baseDelayMs: 500,
+  baseDelayMs: 200,
+  jitter: "full",
   retryOn: ["ETIMEDOUT", "ECONNRESET", 429, 503],
 };

Variants: default (mixed add/del/context); an all-context hunk should use code-panel instead.

Comparison grid

Two to four options laid side by side so the reader picks one — approaches, vendors, plans — each with a short case and a best-for line. The recommended option is marked with a bold run-in prefix in the body text, never a chip or border change.

Managed queue

Hosted broker, autoscaled consumers, dashboards out of the box. Fastest to stand up, least control over retry semantics.

best for: teams without spare ops bandwidth

Self-hosted worker pool

Recommended. Our own retry and backoff logic on top of a plain job table — no new infra, and we already run the failure paths we need.

best for: this migration, given our current retry requirements

Serverless functions

Pay-per-invocation, zero idle cost. Cold starts and a 15-minute execution ceiling rule out our longest ingest jobs.

best for: bursty, short-lived jobs

Variants: 2-up, 3-up, 4-up; all collapse to one column under 680px.

Disclosure

Progressive detail the reader can skip by default — sources, raw data, an appendix, a long log. Built on native details/summary so it works with JS off; never used to hide a document's main conclusion.

Raw latency samples (n=482)

p50 88ms, p90 210ms, p99 640ms, captured across three regions over the seven days preceding the migration. Full CSV export is linked in the appendix.

Sources and further reading

Original incident report · Vendor SLA terms · Internal retry benchmark, June 2026

Variants: default (bordered card) or `.disclosure-plain` for a quiet expandable line.

Timeline

Milestones, phases, or an incident chronology that happened in a real order, where the order itself carries information. The vertical connector is always a hairline, never an accent stripe; markers carry state — filled for done, a hairline ring for pending, accent fill for the current step.

  1. Incident detected 14:02 UTC

    Error rate on checkout-service crossed the 5% alert threshold; on-call paged automatically.

  2. Root cause identified 14:19 UTC

    Bad config push to the payments gateway rejected all requests missing a new required header.

  3. Rollback in progress 14:24 UTC

    Reverting the config to the last known-good revision; checkout traffic is being shed to a static fallback in the meantime.

  4. Postmortem published pending

    Write-up due within 48 hours of resolution per the incident policy.

Variants: default (title + detail + date) or compact (title and date only).

Flow diagram

A branching system where node-edge relationships carry meaning — a dependency map, request path, or state machine. Hand-authored inline SVG on theme tokens, capped at about eight nodes, with exactly one node emphasized and a text caption carrying the same information for accessibility.

A commit triggers a CI build, which runs the test suite; a pass deploys to staging, a fail triggers an automatic rollback.

Variants: none — one node-edge visual language, rearranged per flow.

Wide figure

One focal screenshot or diagram that needs more room than the article column. The figure breaks out to a controlled maximum width, then contracts inside the viewport with a readable point-specific caption.

Variants: breakout (default) or contained in the article column.

Evidence gallery

Two to four screenshots or figures with readable captions, one optionally spanning the grid as the focal piece of evidence. Larger sets should be split by claim instead of compressed into a dense contact sheet.

Variants: equal grid or one featured item.

Pull quote

One thesis line worth isolating from the surrounding prose so the reader can't skim past it — the single claim the rest of the document argues for. Typographic weight and isolation are the only emphasis: no border, no bar, no quotation marks.

The migration is safe to ship precisely because it changes nothing a caller can observe until the compatibility shim is removed in v3. — Migration plan, Decision section

Variants: none — one per document, maximum.

Theme toggle

A reader-facing light/dark switch for artifacts worth revisiting, not one-off scrolls. It's fixed top-right in real use; page-behavior wires the click handler, reading and persisting the theme choice, so this markup only supplies the button and its icon swap.

Variants: none — fixed top-right placement only; the page's real toggle (top-right of this atlas) is a live working instance.

Sources: registry/registry.json and each component file in registry/.
